title: Blacktop Payday (Truck Driving Country genre - shot 1)
caption: Truck driving country genre - untouched pure AI
about: Truck Driving Country with steady mid-tempo two-step groove, brushed drums, twangy Telecaster licks, pedal steel fills, and a walking bass under the verses; verse stays roomy with acoustic guitar and solo vocal, pre-chorus lifts with harmony and snare pickup, chorus opens with gang vocals and a singback hook, Road-noise ear candy, passing-mile markers, and short steel swells between lines, Warm, open mix with a dusty radio feel and a bright, punchy low end, simple, country
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
Fuel gauge low
Sun coming down
Cab smells like coffee
Rolling out of town
Big road ahead
Little pay in my hand
But I got this rig
And a stubborn plan

[Pre-Chorus]
White lines calling
Mile after mile
I been gone all week
Gonna run that stretch awhile

[Chorus]
Keep it rolling, keep it rolling
Down that blacktop line
Keep it rolling, keep it rolling
Gonna make it home on time
Truck stop baby
One more song
Keep it rolling, keep it rolling
Till the dawn is gone

[Verse 2]
CB crackles
Hear my name
Rain on the windshield
Then the sky went tame
She left a note
On the dash by the glove
Said drive safe, baby
I’m waiting with love

[Pre-Chorus]
White lines calling
Mile after mile
I been gone all week
Gonna run that stretch awhile

[Chorus]
Keep it rolling, keep it rolling
Down that blacktop line
Keep it rolling, keep it rolling
Gonna make it home on time
Truck stop baby
One more song
Keep it rolling, keep it rolling
Till the dawn is gone

[Bridge]
When the world gets heavy
And the load runs long
I lean on the wheel
And I sing this song
Every bend, every brake
Every little fight
Leads me back
To her porch light

[Chorus]
Keep it rolling, keep it rolling
Down that blacktop line
Keep it rolling, keep it rolling
Gonna make it home on time
Truck stop baby
One more song
Keep it rolling, keep it rolling
Till the dawn is gone
